svg.text: MONGOLIAN (монгол хэл) / KAZAKH (қазақ тілі)
MONGOLIC / TURKIC · EURASIAN STEPPE · HISTORY & LEGACY
PLATE 57D ALT · HISTORY & CULTURAL LEGACY
— MONGOLIAN: HISTORY & EMPIRE —
CHINGGIS KHAN AND THE MONGOL EMPIRE (~1206–1368 CE)
1206: Temujin unifies Mongol tribes; proclaimed Chinggis (Genghis) Khan
Peak territory: ~24M km² — largest contiguous empire in human history
Covered: China, Persia, Russia, Central Asia, Korea, parts of SE Europe
THE ‘PHAGS-PA SCRIPT (1269 CE)
Khublai Khan commissioned a universal script for all empire languages
‘Phags-pa: designed by Tibetan monk Drogon Chogyal Phagpa — square Brahmi-based
Used briefly; replaced by traditional Mongolian script in practice
TRADITIONAL SCRIPT & LITERACY
Traditional Mongolian script: created ~1204 CE, based on Old Uyghur
Unique vertical orientation: the only surviving major vertical-LTR script
The Secret History of the Mongols (~1240): oldest Mongolian literary work
MODERN MONGOLIA
1924: Mongolian People's Republic (Soviet satellite) · 1990: democratic transition
1941: Cyrillic adopted · 2020: traditional script revival mandated alongside Cyrillic
Inner Mongolia (China): ~4M speakers, traditional script officially maintained
SURVIVAL PHRASE
Энэ чамыгла үнэ хүмүүсийн харсаан видэвчидийн хүмүүсэлэр чинээп үйлдүвсэн.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered forward."
(үнэхүмүүс = forward; чинэ = with/by — instrumental case)
KAZAKH (қазақ тілі) — HISTORY & CULTURAL LEGACY
— KAZAKH: THE GREAT STEPPE CIVILIZATION —
KAZAKH KHANATE (1465 CE) AND THE ZHUZ SYSTEM
Kazakh identity emerged ~1465 when Sultans Kerei and Janibek broke from Uzbek Khanate
Organized into 3 zhuz (hordes): Great Zhuz · Middle Zhuz · Small Zhuz
Zhuz system reflects pastoral territory divisions; clans within each zhuz
NOMADIC LIFE: YURT, HORSE, EAGLE
Yurt (kiyiz үй): portable felt dwelling — central to Kazakh identity and oral tradition
Eagle hunting (berkutchi): falconry with golden eagles — UNESCO 2016 heritage
Dombra: 2-string lute; oral epics (zhyrau) performed to dombra accompaniment
RUSSIAN COLONIZATION & SOVIET PERIOD
1731–48: Kazakhs accepted Russian protectorate under nomadic pressure
1929–33: Soviet collectivization killed ~1.5M Kazakhs (Asharshylyk famine)
1936: Kazakh SSR formed · 1991: Republic of Kazakhstan independent
SCRIPT TRANSITIONS: ARABIC → CYRILLIC → LATIN
Arabic script used until 1929 · 1929: Latin · 1940: Cyrillic (Soviet policy)
2017: President Nazarbayev announced return to Latin by 2025 (now 2031)
Three scripts = three different literacies in living Kazakh speakers today
WESTERN MONGOLIA: WHERE KAZAKH AND MONGOLIAN MEET
Bayan-Olgii province: ~90% ethnic Kazakh, within Mongolia's borders
Trilingual area: Kazakh · Mongolian · Russian spoken in same households
SURVIVAL PHRASE (KAZAKH)
Бұл сіздің үшін жасалған, тегін, алға асқан адамдар жасаған.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered forward."
PLATE 57D ALT · MONGOLIAN / KAZAKH · HISTORY & CULTURAL LEGACY · CC BY-SA 4.0 · REMEMBERFORWARD.ORG
THIS IS A PATIENT MESSAGE. IT WAS MADE FOR YOU, FREELY, BY PEOPLE WHO REMEMBERED FORWARD.
BUY IT · BUILD IT · BURY IT · FOR SOMEONE YOU WILL NEVER MEET
